prose:
Frontier-orbital energies for water (H₂O) computed at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet), using PySCF as the backend. The geometry was taken from the user-supplied file at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz and was not re-optimized (single-point on the supplied geometry). SCF converged in 7 cycles. Integrity checks: trustworthy = true, all checks passed. No warnings were reported (clean run).

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV.
HOMO energy = −8.6037183 eV (orbital index 4, occupied, 5 total occupied orbitals).
LUMO energy = +0.33695037 eV (orbital index 5, unoccupied).
Total electronic energy = −76.462901 Hartree (−2080.6615 eV).

Koopmans-derived properties: vertical IP = 8.6037 eV, vertical EA = −0.33695 eV, electronegativity = 4.1334 eV, chemical hardness = 4.4703 eV.

Method: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, density-fitted), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard [input.xyz]. Output: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpcs5kmevn/live.json. Warnings array: empty (no warnings). Provenance: computed.
